memoryArtifacts.description: Hugh le  Despencer, Chief Justiciar of England, first played an important part in 1258, when he was prominent on the baronial side in the Mad Parliament of Oxford.  In 1260 the barons chose him to suc …

memoryArtifacts.headline: Epitaph
memoryArtifacts.description:
Sprung from the noble and the brave,
Here Mabel finds a narrow grave.
But, above all woman's glory,
Fills a page in famous story.
Commanding, eloquent, and wise,
And prompt to daring enterprise; …

zh.lifeSummary: Hugh Le Despenser是Sir Hugh le Despenser和Mary de Quincy的兒子，於1223年8月5日出生在英國萊希斯特斯爾洛賀波洛。他於1244年之前與Aline Basset在英國結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他於1244年登記服兵役。他於1260年被列為justicar of england。他去世於1265年8月4日在英國武斯特斯爾艾維沙安享年41歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾艾維沙安 哥吉拉 沙智 煙雨 聖 勞倫斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Hugh Le Despenser是Sir Hugh le Despenser和Mary de Quincy的儿子，于1223年8月5日出生在英国萊希斯特斯爾洛賀波洛。他于1244年之前与Aline Basset在英国结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他于1244年登记服兵役。他于1260年被列为justicar of england。他去世于1265年8月4日在英国武斯特斯爾艾維沙安享年41岁，埋葬在英国英格兰武斯特斯爾艾維沙安 哥吉拉 沙智 煙雨 聖 勞倫斯。

nameMeanings.name: Le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Hugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Hugues, Hugo, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’. It was originally a short form of various compound names containing this element. This was borne by the aristocracy of medieval France, adopted by the Normans, and introduced by them to Britain. It was consistently one of the most popular boys' names in the Middle Ages in England, but fell out of favour in the 16th century until it was revived in the 20th century. Little Hugh of Lincoln was a child supposed in the Middle Ages to have been murdered by Jews in about 1255 , a legend responsible for several outbursts of anti-Semitism at various times. The story is referred to by Chaucer in The Prioress's Tale. He is not to be confused with St Hugh of Lincoln ( 1140–1200 ), bishop of Lincoln ( 1186–1200 ), who was noted for his charity and good works, his piety, and his defence of the Church against the State. Since the later Middle Ages Hugh has also been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Aodh .

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: Hugh Le Despenser was born on 5 August 1223, in Loughborough, Leicestershire, England as the son of Sir Hugh le Despenser and Mary de Quincy. He married Aline Basset before 1244, in England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. He registered for military service in 1244. In 1260, at the age of 37, his occupation is listed as justicar of england. He died on 4 August 1265, in Evesham, Worcestershire, England, at the age of 41, and was buried in Evesham All Saints with St Lawrence, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Hugh Le Despenser est né le 5 août 1223, à Loughborough, Leicestershire, Angleterre, fils de Sir Hugh le Despenser et Mary de Quincy. Il a épousé Aline Basset avant 1244, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1244. En 1260, à l’âge de 37 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est justicar of england. Il est décédé le 4 août 1265, à Evesham, Worcestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 41 ans, et a été enterré à Evesham All Saints with St Lawrence, Worcestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Hugh Le Despenser wurde am 5. August 1223 in Loughborough, Leicestershire, England, als Sohn von Sir Hugh le Despenser und Mary de Quincy geboren. Er hat vor 1244 in England Aline Basset geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er hat sich 1244 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. 1260, als er 37 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als justicar of england aufgeführt. Er starb am 4. August 1265 im Alter von 41 Jahren in Evesham, Worcestershire, England und wurde in Evesham All Saints with St Lawrence, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Hugh Le Despenser è nato il il 5 agosto 1223 nel Loughborough, Leicestershire, England come figlio di Sir Hugh le Despenser e Mary de Quincy. Lui sposato Aline Basset prima del 1244, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 3 figlie. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1244. Nel 1260, all'età di 37 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come justicar of england. Morì il 4 agosto 1265, nel Evesham, Worcestershire, England, all'età di 41 anni, e fu sepolto a Evesham All Saints with St Lawrence, Worcestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Hugh Le Despenserは1223年8月5日にイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアロウボロウでSir Hugh le DespenserとMary de Quincyの息子として生まれた。1244年以前にイギリスでAline Bassetと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1244年に兵役登録をした。1260年，37歳の時，職業はjusticar of englandと記録されている。彼は1265年8月4日にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアエベシャムで41歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアエベシャム アル サインツ ウィス スト ラウレンスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Hugh Le Despenser(은)는 1223년 8월 5일 영국 레이스스터실르 루그h버루에서 Sir Hugh le Despenser(와)과 Mary de Quincy의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1244년 이전 영국에서 Aline Basset(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1244년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 1260년, 37세의 나이로 그의 직업은 justicar of england이었다. 그는 1265년 8월 4일 영국 워스스터실르 에베샤에서 41세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르 에베샤 앨 세인츠 위스 스트 로렌스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Hugh Le Despenser nasceu em 5 de agosto de 1223, em Loughborough, Leicestershire, Inglaterra filho de Sir Hugh le Despenser e Mary de Quincy. Ele casou-se com Aline Basset antes 1244, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1244. Em 1260, com 37 anos, sua profissão aparece como justicar of england. Ele faleceu em 4 de agosto de 1265, em Evesham, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, com 41 anos, e foi sepultado em Evesham All Saints with St Lawrence, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Hugh Le Despenser родился 5 августа 1223 г. в местности лоугбороую, лисестиршир, Англия. Его родителей звали Sir Hugh le Despenser и Mary de Quincy. Он женился на женщине по имени Aline Basset перед 1244 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. В 1244 году он поступил на военную службу. В 1260 году, в возрасте 37 лет, работал по профессии justicar of england. Он умер 4 августа 1265 г. в местности эвешам, ворсестиршир, Англия в возрасте 41 год и был похоронен в местности эвешам алл сеинц вит ст ларенс, ворсестиршир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Hugh Le Despenser nació el 5 de agosto de 1223, en Loughborough, Leicestershire, Inglaterra como hijo de Sir Hugh le Despenser y Mary de Quincy. Se casó con Aline Basset antes de 1244, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 3 hijas. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1244. En 1260, a la edad de 37 años, su ocupación registrada era justicar of england. Murió el 4 de agosto de 1265, en Evesham, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 41 años, y fue sepultado en Evesham All Saints with St Lawrence, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
